what_it_does: This file is an orienteering terrain-entry ledger record that describes six loose Markdown files in /home/tim and frames them as cross-session build handoff and merge-coordination notes for the company's engine, voice/STT, UI, decision-wire, and concurrent-cognition work.
observations: Six Markdown files are loose directly in /home/tim, not inside a folder, totaling ~76K.
The files record historical provenance of how parallel Claude Code sessions built and merged work onto main.
A human relay protocol is described: Tim physically carried file paths between sessions that did not know the other existed.
Specific branch names, worktree paths, merge-base commits, and HEAD commits (e.g., ca84ddc) are referenced.
One file documents a foundational vocabulary mismatch between a blueprint vocabulary and the actual engine record types.
One file lists a 7-file conflict surface, highlighting runtime/suite.py chat() refactor and canvas/app/src/useAppController.ts as hard merges.
The voice streaming protocol POST /api/voice/stream is documented with a low-latency chunking strategy.
The STT file documents a swappable ear registry of six providers with config-slot selection and fail-loud no-fallback behavior.
Coverage caveat: all six heads were read, but only voice-stt-handoff and round3-grounding-report were read in substantial depth.
The entry has no git remote, status is unconfirmed, and secrets are marked false.
standouts: The explicit human relay protocol used to coordinate sessions that were unaware of each other.
The 7-file conflict surface and proposed merge division for concurrent-cognition work.
The voice streaming protocol that plays chunk idx=0 while idx=1 synthesizes, cutting latency.
The STT ear registry design with six swappable providers and intentional no-fallback behavior.
The documented vocabulary mismatch between the blueprint and the actual engine record types.
concerns: Files are loose in /home/tim rather than organized in a folder or repository, making them easy to misplace.
No git remote is recorded, so version history and authorship are not machine-verifiable from this entry.
Status is marked unconfirmed despite the entry asserting the files were read.
Branch and HEAD references may now be stale, but the entry does not indicate when they were last validated.
Coverage caveat means some files were only skimmed, so details may be incomplete.
The provenance chain depends on a human relay step, which is not reproducible from the file alone.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, register, aliases, path, relation, kind, status, dates, size, coverage, git_remote, relates-to, tags, and custom doc_kind fields.
Uses Markdown headings and bullet lists to describe content and relationships.
Records absolute file paths and byte sizes for the six constituent files.
One referenced file uses an append-only REPLY section for cross-session dialogue.
Another referenced file uses OBSERVED/INFERRED/VERIFIED evidence labels.
No code symbols are extracted; the entry is purely prose provenance.
notes: A grouped ledger entry that maps six loose Markdown handoff files in /home/tim and explains their role as the cross-session merge-coordination provenance for engine/voice/UI/wire/cognition work.
questions: Are the merges these documents coordinated fully complete and verified on current main?
Do the referenced worktrees and branch states still exist in the same form?
Was the foundational vocabulary mismatch between the blueprint and engine record types ever resolved?
Why do these historically important coordination files remain loose in /home/tim instead of being archived in a repository?
What is the current main HEAD, and how does it relate to the ca84ddc reference?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it is a cross-session build handoff and merge-coordination record, and its content indeed describes the six files, their seams, conflicts, contracts, and relay narrative.
apparent_themes: Merge provenance and conflict surface documentation
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to preserve the narrative of how parallel build sessions converged onto main and to prevent silent loss of work by explicitly documenting seams, conflict surfaces, division of labor, and contracts.
novelty: The unusual aspect is the meta-recording of a human relay protocol between unaware Claude Code sessions, treating cross-session merge coordination itself as orienteering terrain worth indexing.
contribution: It contributes a provenance index that locates six handoff documents, records their absolute paths, dates, sizes, conflict surfaces, streaming and registry contracts, and the cross-session relay narrative, enabling future queries to reconstruct how the engine/voice/UI/wire/cognition work was merged.
summary_for_embedding: An orienteering terrain-entry describing six loose Markdown files in /home/tim that serve as cross-session build handoff and merge-coordination notes for the company's engine, voice/STT, UI, decision-wire, and concurrent-cognition work.
intention_for_embedding: To record the provenance and seam surfaces of parallel Claude Code sessions so merges onto main could be coordinated without silent loss of work.
